framework.name: Neural Inverse Enterprise Security Standard
framework.description: Production-grade security, reliability, and architecture framework showcasing all GRC engine capabilities including nano-agent-powered checks, context-aware regex, inter-procedural dataflow, and import graph enforcement.

rules.title: No eval() or Function() constructor
rules.description: eval() and new Function() execute arbitrary strings as code, enabling code injection attacks. Context-aware: skips matches in comments and strings.
rules.fix: Replace eval() with JSON.parse() for data, or use a sandboxed execution context.

rules.title: No hardcoded secrets or API keys
rules.description: Hardcoded credentials expose sensitive data in source control.
rules.fix: Use environment variables or a secrets manager.

rules.title: No console.log in production code
rules.description: Console statements can leak sensitive information. Skips matches in comments.
rules.fix: Use a structured logging library with log levels.

rules.title: No innerHTML assignment
rules.description: Direct innerHTML assignment enables XSS. Context-aware: only flags in code, not comments.
rules.fix: Use textContent for plain text, or DOMPurify.sanitize() before setting innerHTML.

rules.title: No TODO/FIXME/HACK in production
rules.description: Unresolved TODO comments indicate incomplete implementation.
rules.fix: Resolve the TODO or convert it to a tracked issue.

rules.title: No multi-line SQL string concatenation
rules.description: Multi-line SQL strings built with concatenation are prone to injection. Uses multiline matching to detect across line breaks.
rules.fix: Use parameterized queries or an ORM.

rules.title: Async functions must have try/catch
rules.description: Unhandled promise rejections in async functions can crash the process.
rules.fix: Wrap the async function body in try/catch.

rules.title: Functions should not exceed 5 parameters
rules.description: Functions with many parameters indicate need for refactoring.
rules.fix: Use an options/config object pattern.

rules.title: Crypto operations require error handling
rules.description: Files using crypto must wrap operations in try/catch. Powered by nano agent capability detection.
rules.fix: Add try/catch around all crypto operations.

rules.title: Network code must have auth checks
rules.description: Files making network requests should include authentication logic. Powered by nano agent capability detection.
rules.fix: Add authentication middleware or token validation.

rules.title: Async functions must use await
rules.description: Async functions without await are likely missing intended async operations.
rules.fix: Add await calls or remove the async keyword.

rules.title: Error handlers must re-throw or log
rules.description: Functions that catch errors should either throw or call a logging function.
rules.fix: Add error logging or re-throw the error in catch blocks.

rules.title: High complexity files need review
rules.description: Files with deep nesting (complexity > 8) are harder to maintain.
rules.fix: Extract nested logic into helper functions.

rules.title: Env access must have validation
rules.description: Direct process.env access without error handling can cause undefined behavior.
rules.fix: Validate environment variables at startup using a config validation library.

rules.title: SQL injection: unsanitized input in database queries
rules.description: User input flowing directly into database queries enables SQL injection. Now tracks through destructuring, template literals, and cross-function returns.
rules.fix: Use parameterized queries or an ORM.

rules.title: XSS: unsanitized input in response
rules.description: User input sent directly to HTTP responses enables XSS.
rules.fix: Sanitize all user input before including it in responses.

rules.title: Command injection: unsanitized input in shell
rules.description: User input flowing into child_process enables OS command injection.
rules.fix: Use child_process.execFile() with explicit arguments array.

rules.title: Path traversal: unsanitized input in file operations
rules.description: User input flowing into file system calls enables path traversal attacks.
rules.fix: Validate and sanitize file paths. Use path.basename() to strip directory traversal.

rules.title: Files must not exceed 800 lines
rules.description: Large files are hard to review, test, and maintain.
rules.fix: Split into smaller, focused modules.

rules.title: Source files must have copyright header
rules.description: All source files must include the organization's copyright header.
rules.fix: Add the standard copyright header.

rules.title: UI layer must not import from data layer
rules.description: UI components should only import from allowed layers. Enforces clean architecture.
rules.fix: Access data through the services layer.

rules.title: No circular imports
rules.description: Circular dependencies cause runtime issues and indicate poor module design.
rules.fix: Extract shared types/interfaces to a separate module.
